svg.text: Architectural Design — Platform Integration in Enterprise Security Workflow
How the AI Threat Hunting Platform augments existing detection systems — not a replacement, an enhancement layer
Physical and logical assets generating security-relevant telemetry
Rule-based, signature-based detection — effective against known threats, blind to novel APT techniques
⚠ GAP: These tools CANNOT detect credential-based lateral movement using valid credentials or covert DNS exfiltration — attackers use legitimate tools ("living off the land")
LAYER 3: Endpoint Telemetry Collection (Deployed by This Project)
Deep visibility layer — captures what existing security tools miss
EID 1: Process creation • EID 3: Network connection
EID 10: LSASS access (credential dump) • EID 22: DNS query
Fills visibility gaps that AV/EDR misses
Authentication events for lateral movement detection
Ships encrypted (AES-256) to Wazuh Manager on port 1514
Deployed via GPO or PowerShell script across all endpoints
Firewall logs, IDS/IPS alerts, proxy logs
also forwarded to Wazuh via syslog/Filebeat
(existing integration — not new to this project)
LAYER 4: Wazuh SIEM — Central Log Aggregation & Correlation
Already deployed in enterprise • Rule-based detection + log indexing • REST API enables AI Platform integration
Effective but blind to novel TTPs
LAYER 5: ⭐ AI Threat Hunting Platform (This Project) — Augments Layer 4
ML-based behavioral detection of threats that rule-based systems cannot see
🔒 Security Layer: RBAC (4 roles, 11 permissions) • JWT/API-Key Auth • Model Integrity (HMAC-SHA256) • Hash-Chained Audit Trail • Input Sanitization (6-layer)
Event Bus (async pub/sub) • Structured JSON Logging (correlation IDs) • Health Checks • Drift Monitoring • Dead Letter Queue
NormalizedEvent → FeatureVector → Detection → EnrichedAlert → Wazuh Dashboard + Attack Graph (Pydantic schema contracts between every module)
Deployed as Docker containers: api + visualization on internal-net • All configs via YAML • docker compose up -d
What This Layer Detects That Layers 2–4 Cannot:
✓ Credential-based lateral movement using valid accounts (Pass-the-Hash, Kerberos abuse) — invisible to signature-based tools
✓ Covert DNS exfiltration (dns tunneling via high-entropy queries, TXT record abuse) — bypasses firewalls entirely
🔒 DP ε=1.0 • Trust scoring • Byzantine tolerance
No raw data crosses org boundary
LAYER 7: Security Operations Center (SOC) — Human Decision Layer
Analysts receive AI-enriched alerts alongside existing Wazuh alerts — unified view, better context, faster response
AI-enriched alerts + attack graphs + SHAP explanations ↑
